name: dumbbell one arm bench fly
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on top of your thigh.
Lie back on the bench, keeping the dumbbell pressed against your thigh.
Using your free hand, help lift the dumbbell up to the starting position.
Hold the dumbbell directly above your shoulder with your arm extended and palm facing inward.
Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on top of your thigh. Lie back on the bench, keeping the dumbbell pressed against your thigh. Using your free hand, help lift the dumbbell up to the starting position. Hold the dumbbell directly above your shoulder with your arm extended and palm facing inward. Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow. Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbell back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Avoid supine positions after the first trimester due to vena cava compression. Incline or side-lie alternatives preferred.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
